# @hoge1e3/ref-input

Binds a `Ref` (or `PathRef`) to an HTML input element, keeping the ref value and the input's displayed value in **two-way sync**.

- When `ref.value` changes, the input is updated in the DOM.
- When the user edits the input, `ref.value` is updated.

Works with both text inputs and checkboxes.

## Installation

```sh
npm install @hoge1e3/ref-input
```

## API

### `refInput<T extends HTMLElement>(ref, input): T`

```typescript
export function refInput<T extends HTMLElement>(ref: any, input: T): T;
```

**Parameters:**
- `ref` — A `Ref` or `PathRef` (from `@hoge1e3/ref` or `@hoge1e3/immer-path-ref`) to bind to.
- `input` — An `HTMLInputElement` (or compatible element) that **must have an `id` attribute set**. The `id` is used to look up the element in the DOM when the ref changes.

**Returns:** The same `input` element, for convenient inline use.

**Throws:** If the element does not have an `id` attribute.

## Behavior

- On call, immediately sets the input's current value from `ref.value`.
- Registers an `"input"` event listener: when the user types or toggles, `ref.value` is updated.
- Registers a `ref.on("change", ...)` listener: when `ref.value` changes programmatically, the DOM element is looked up by `id` and updated.
- For **checkboxes** (`input.type === "checkbox"`): syncs `checked` ↔ `boolean`.
- For **text inputs** and others: syncs `value` ↔ `string`.

## Usage

### Text input

```js
import { ref } from "@hoge1e3/ref";
import { t } from "@hoge1e3/dom";
import { id } from "@hoge1e3/dom-id";
import { refInput } from "@hoge1e3/ref-input";

const wid = id("form");
const name = ref("Alice");

const input = refInput(name, t.input({ id: wid.name + "", type: "text" }));
document.body.appendChild(input);

// Programmatic update → reflected in DOM
name.value = "Bob"; // input now shows "Bob"

// User types "Carol" → ref is updated
// name.value === "Carol"
```

### Checkbox

```js
const showDone = ref(false);

const checkbox = refInput(showDone, t.input({ id: wid.showDone + "", type: "checkbox" }));
document.body.appendChild(checkbox);

showDone.value = true; // checkbox becomes checked
```

### Inline with `@hoge1e3/dom` and `@hoge1e3/component`

`refInput` is designed to be used inline within component render functions:

```js
import { c } from "@hoge1e3/component";
import { refInput } from "@hoge1e3/ref-input";

const el = c(wid.root, titleRef, (title) =>
  t.div(
    refInput(titleRef, t.input({ id: wid.title + "", type: "text" })),
    t.span(`Current: ${title}`)
  )
);
```

## Notes

- The element **must have an `id` attribute** set before passing to `refInput`. Use `@hoge1e3/dom-id` to generate stable IDs.
- Each `(ref, id)` pair is only registered once — calling `refInput` multiple times with the same ref and element ID is safe and idempotent.
- Dependencies: `@hoge1e3/dom-mutable-events`
